Title: Natural variation in tetrapyrrole biosynthetic enzymes and their regulation modifies the maize chlorophyll mutant Oy1-N1989

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ELM2 transcript abundance at a hotspot SNP was associated with lower chlorophyll content in Oy1-N1989 mutants and higher chlorophyll in WT siblings, indicating crosstalk between heme/bilin and chlorophyll pathways
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A hotspot SNP in L3Base was associated with lower ELM2 transcript accumulation. 2. The same allele decreased chlorophyll in Oy1-N1989 mutants but increased WT chlorophyll-related difference traits. 3. This supports regulatory crosstalk between the heme/bilin and chlorophyll branches.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Trans-eQTL hotspot overlap with chlorophyll trait GWAS
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Diverse maize lines and Oy1-N1989 F1 families
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper references prior elm1 mutant interaction work, but in this study the evidence is from natural variation affecting elm2 transcript levels.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A hotspot SNP linked to reduced LES22L2 expression increased chlorophyll accumulation in Oy1-N1989 mutants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNP 5-211181311 in an LMAD hotspot affected les22l2 expression. 2. The allele decreasing LES22L2 transcript abundance increased mutant chlorophyll traits. 3. The role of les22-like genes remains unresolved, highlighting a gap in understanding green-plant uroporphyrinogen decarboxylase paralogs.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Trans-eQTL hotspot overlap with chlorophyll trait GWAS; phylogenetic analysis discussion
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Diverse maize lines and Oy1-N1989 F1 families
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The biochemical role of les22-like paralogs is uncertain; Arabidopsis HEME1/HEME2 orthologs reportedly fail to complement yeast mutants.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step / branch point into chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent Mg2+ insertion into protoporphyrin IX by the Mg-chelatase complex, committing tetrapyrrole flux to chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms committed-step intermediate for chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established committed-step enzyme for chlorophyll biosynthesis; key control point supported
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced OY1 function lowers chlorophyll accumulation; higher wild-type oy1 expression suppresses severity of Oy1-N1989, while lower expression enhances chlorophyll deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. oy1 encodes CHLI, the subunit mutated in semi-dominant Oy1-N1989. 2. Natural cis-regulatory variation at wild-type oy1 modulates OY1 transcript abundance and thereby alters mutant chlorophyll severity. 3. oy1-linked natural variation also acts in trans on multiple tetrapyrrole genes, indicating pathway-level feedback regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of Oy1-N1989 heterozygotes; chlorophyll phenotyping; RNA-seq; cis-eGWAS; trans-eGWAS; association with mutant chlorophyll traits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73; Mo17 introgression line b094; B73 × b094 F1-derived materials
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaf tissues including L3Base, mature leaves during day (LMAD), and mature leaves during night (LMAN)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA1321763); eGWAS using transcriptome data from 296 maize lines; GWAS for chlorophyll traits in 343 F1 families
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AAA+ ATPase family Mg-chelatase subunit I
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Oy1-N1989 is a semi-dominant dominant-negative allele. The paper emphasizes expression variation at the wild-type oy1 allele (vey1 cis-regulatory polymorphism) as the major modifier of mutant severity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced oy1 expression causes chlorosis and developmental defects due to impaired chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Cis-regulatory tuning of oy1 can modulate chlorophyll accumulation; useful for understanding chlorophyll robustness but severe reduction is detrimental and should be avoided in breeding

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step / branch point into chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with CHLI and CHLD in Mg-chelatase-mediated insertion of Mg2+ into protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly participates in committed-step formation of chlorophyll precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control complex component, not independently established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CHLH1 transcript accumulation is associated with increased chlorophyll content in Oy1-N1989 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chlh1 transcripts were reduced in the severe Oy1-N1989/oy1Mo17 background. 2. Cis-regulatory variation at chlh1 affected mutant chlorophyll content but not WT chlorophyll content. 3. Increased CHLH1 expression was associated with increased chlorophyll accumulation in the mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; cis-eGWAS; association overlap with chlorophyll traits
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73; Mo17 introgression line b094; F1 materials
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in maize leaf tissues used for eGWAS and RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its effect on chlorophyll was cryptic in WT but exposed in the Oy1-N1989 sensitized background.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step / branch point into chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with CHLI and CHLH in Mg-chelatase to insert Mg2+ into protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly participates in committed-step formation of chlorophyll precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control complex component, not independently established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript abundance increases in Oy1-N1989 mutants, consistent with compensatory transcriptional feedback at Mg-chelatase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chld1 transcripts increased in Oy1-N1989 mutants. 2. This contrasted with repression of many other chlorophyll pathway genes. 3. chld1 was repeatedly targeted by trans-eQTL hotspots and oy1-linked trans-regulation, indicating coordinated pathway feedback.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; trans-eGWAS hotspot analysis; chlorophyll biosynthesis index GWAS
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73; Mo17 introgression line b094; F1 materials
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in L3Base, LMAD, and/or LMAN leaf tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as part of compensatory feedback to reduced Mg-chelatase function rather than as a direct causal polymorphism for chlorophyll content in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate late precursor to chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced pcr1 expression is associated with reduced mutant chlorophyll content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pcr1 transcripts decreased in both Oy1-N1989 mutant backgrounds. 2. Cis polymorphisms reducing pcr1 expression in multiple leaf tissues were associated with lower mutant chlorophyll content. 3. pcr1 therefore contributes to natural variation in chlorophyll accumulation in the sensitized mutant background.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; cis-eGWAS; trait association overlap
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73; Mo17 introgression line b094; F1 materials
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in L3Base, LMAD, and LMAN leaf tissues

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate late precursor to chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher pcr3 expression is associated with increased chlorophyll content in Oy1-N1989 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pcr3 transcripts were reduced in the severe mutant background. 2. Cis SNPs associated with increased pcr3 expression in LMAD increased mutant chlorophyll content. 3. pcr3 was also frequently included among trans-regulated chlorophyll pathway targets.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; cis-eGWAS; trait association overlap; trans-eGWAS hotspot analysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73; Mo17 introgression line b094; F1 materials
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in mature leaves and leaf base tissues

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Common porphyrin pathway upstream of protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts uroporphyrinogen III to coproporphyrinogen III by decarboxylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for chlorophyll branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Unexpectedly, alleles increasing LES22 transcript abundance were associated with reduced chlorophyll content in Oy1-N1989 mutants, suggesting feedback effects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. les22 was one of the porphyrin pathway genes whose cis variation affected mutant chlorophyll content. 2. Increased LES22 expression in LMAD/LMAN correlated with lower chlorophyll in mutants. 3. The inverse relationship was interpreted as evidence for feedback regulation rather than simple precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cis-eGWAS; overlap with mutant chlorophyll trait GWAS
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Maize diversity panel and Oy1-N1989 F1 families
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in mature leaf tissues LMAD and LMAN
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Relationship to chlorophyll is inverse to expectation, highlighting pathway feedback.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes chlorophyll to chlorophyllide during chlorophyll degradation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target metabolite by promoting degradation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced chph2 expression is associated with higher chlorophyll accumulation in both mutant and WT plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cis polymorphisms reducing chph2 expression increased chlorophyll accumulation. 2. This inverse relationship is consistent with chlorophyll catabolism lowering chlorophyll content. 3. Unlike several biosynthetic genes, the effect was observed in both mutant and WT plants.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Maize diversity panel and Oy1-N1989 F1 families
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper explicitly links cis-regulatory variation at chph2 to chlorophyll accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Heme branch competing for protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inserts Fe2+ into protoporphyrin IX to form heme
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch for shared precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch competition point; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alleles increasing fc2 expression were associated with reduced chlorophyll content in mutants, consistent with precursor competition between heme and chlorophyll branches
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cis polymorphisms at fc2 affected mutant chlorophyll content. 2. Increased fc2 expression negatively impacted chlorophyll accumulation. 3. The result is consistent with competition between heme and chlorophyll branches for protoporphyrin IX.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Maize diversity panel and Oy1-N1989 F1 families

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Cis-regulated pathway control locus / local trans-regulatory trigger
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Natural cis-regulatory variation alters OY1 transcript abundance and is associated with trans-acting coordinated regulation of tetrapyrrole pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major determinant of Oy1-N1989 chlorophyll mutant severity and a recurrent trans-eQTL hotspot affecting chlorophyll pathway expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: gsa1; cf1; les22; chld1; gun4; mpmt1; mpec1; ycf54; pcr3; ggh1; chlg1; nyc1l1 and other tetrapyrrole genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: oy1-linked SNPs coordinately altered expression of multiple porphyrin and chlorophyll biosynthetic genes in trans; effect direction often concordant across targets
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Natural allelic variation at oy1/vey1; mutant state Oy1-N1989
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Strong evidence for pathway feedback regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher OY1 expression suppresses chlorophyll deficiency; oy1-linked variation influences pathway-wide transcriptional states
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. oy1 is the key genetic locus modulating chlorophyll accumulation in Oy1-N1989. 2. Cis variation at oy1 correlates with mutant chlorophyll content. 3. oy1-linked trans-eQTL repeatedly target many tetrapyrrole genes, revealing a transcriptional checkpoint.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; cis-eGWAS; trans-eGWAS; hotspot analysis; chlorophyll trait GWAS
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73; Mo17 introgression line b094; diverse maize lines; F1 families
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissues including L3Base, LMAD, LMAN
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although oy1 encodes an enzyme, this paper also establishes it as a trans-acting regulatory locus for pathway-level expression variation; included here because regulatory function is central to results.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning oy1 expression offers a lever to alter chlorophyll robustness, but strong reduction causes severe chlorosis and developmental penalties
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower OY1 causes chlorophyll deficiency and growth defects
